zone_6b_notes: Dependable perennial salvia hardy in Zone 4â€“9 â€” thrives in Zone 6b Farmington. Deep violet-blue flower spikes bloom May through June; deadhead spent spikes promptly to encourage 2â€“3 additional flushes through September. Exceptionally heat and drought tolerant once established â€” a perfect fit for Missouri summers. Shear back to 4 inches after first bloom for best rebloom. Attracts hummingbirds, bumblebees, and butterflies throughout the season. Never needs dividing. Pairs beautifully with yellow and orange flowers (coreopsis, rudbeckia, hemerocallis).

planting.best_planting_time: Plant divisions or nursery stock in spring or fall
